task_description: Assess the risks of adopting a new AI/ML model for automated credit scoring decisions at a retail bank.

risk_factors.name: Model bias
risk_factors.description: Training data may reflect historical lending biases against protected classes

risk_factors.name: Regulatory scrutiny
risk_factors.description: Fair lending laws require explainability; model is partially opaque

risk_factors.name: Data quality
risk_factors.description: 15% of historical records have incomplete fields used as features

risk_factors.name: Concept drift
risk_factors.description: Economic conditions may shift, invalidating model assumptions

risk_factors.name: Operational risk
risk_factors.description: Model inference latency must stay under 200ms for real-time decisions

risk_factors.name: Fallback system
risk_factors.description: Legacy scoring system will be decommissioned 2 months after launch

risk_factors.name: Adversarial inputs
risk_factors.description: Applicants may learn to game model features

input_data.context: Retail bank processing $2B in annual consumer lending
